headline: Ostrich Towne: three tenants now open, CuriosiTEA the next opening to watch
paragraphs: Ostrich Towne now has three operating tenants: Molly's Irish Pub, Avestruz Tapas & Tequila, and Riverside Barbeque Company, which held its grand opening in September 2025 at 915 N. Summit St. Riverside BBQ offers Texas-style smoked meats, a full bar, and live music in a converted beer distribution warehouse — a venue format well-suited to the district's industrial bones. CuriosiTEA Emporium remains the next expected opening, though no confirmed date has surfaced this window.

With three tenants open and Watershed Weekend foot traffic expected on the adjacent MLK Bridge in June, the district enters summer 2026 at a meaningfully different baseline than a year ago. The question for this season is whether the three-anchor cluster is enough to build dwell-time and return visits without a fourth or fifth tenant to extend the visit loop.
why_it_matters: The Ostrich Towne thesis has always been 'anchor tenants plus Riverwalk tailwind equals a destination district.' Riverside BBQ's survival through its first winter confirms the first leg of that thesis. The watch item has shifted: CuriosiTEA's opening date is now the leading indicator for whether the district can sustain enough variety to pull repeat visitors. Watch the Vibrancy 19 property list — if any of the 19 awards land inside Vistula, the leasing pitch to the next wave of tenants gets materially easier.
search_terms: Ostrich Towne Vistula Toledo Riverside BBQ CuriosiTEA Molly's Avestruz tenants open
